metric_definition.description: Retention at 10% into the video — measures hook strength. How many people are still watching 1/10 of the way through?
metric_definition.what_to_extract: retentionCurve[10].retention — the retention value at index 10 (= 10% through video)

result.conclusion: No meaningful linear relationship found between hook_retention_pct and views (r=-0.050, 95% CI [-0.151, +0.052], p=0.3379, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.121 also confirms no rank-order relationship. This indicator does not predict view count in Tyler's corpus of 370 videos.
result.practical_insight: Retention at 10% into the video does not meaningfully predict views. Safe to deprioritize.

metric_definition.description: Average retention in the final 5% of the video — end-completion signal. Videos that hold viewers to the very end.

result.conclusion: Average retention in the final 5% of the video — end-completion signal. Videos that hold viewers to the very end. Is moderately positively correlated with views (r=+0.472, 95% CI [+0.388, +0.547], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.472 confirms a linear relationship. Higher final_5pct_retention values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Maximize average retention in the final 5% of the video to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Largest single-step drop in retention anywhere in the video — the worst moment of viewer loss.
metric_definition.what_to_extract: max(|curve[i] - curve[i-1]|) for all consecutive pairs

result.conclusion: Largest single-step drop in retention anywhere in the video — the worst moment of viewer loss. Is weakly positively correlated with views (r=+0.177, 95% CI [+0.076, +0.274], p=0.0006, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.442 confirms a partially non-linear relationship. Higher mid_video_cliff values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Maximize largest single-step drop in retention anywhere in the video to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Shannon entropy of the retention curve — information richness. High entropy means many peaks/valleys vs monotonic decline.

result.conclusion: Shannon entropy of the retention curve — information richness. High entropy means many peaks/valleys vs monotonic decline. Is moderately positively correlated with views (r=+0.465, 95% CI [+0.381, +0.541], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=+0.556 confirms a linear relationship. Higher retention_entropy values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Maximize shannon entropy of the retention curve to increase views.

metric_definition.description: Slope of retention in the first 10% of video. More negative = viewers dropping faster right after start.

result.conclusion: Slope of retention in the first 10% of video. More negative = viewers dropping faster right after start. Is moderately negatively correlated with views (r=-0.402, 95% CI [-0.484, -0.313], p=0.0000, n=370). Spearman rho=-0.391 confirms a linear relationship. Lower hook_drop_rate values predict more views.
result.practical_insight: Minimize slope of retention in the first 10% of video. more negative = viewers dropping faster right after start. to increase views.
